formations_blocks: Jordan defending deep and then breaking quickly in transition
Algeria circulating possession at the back before attacking at pace
Algeria pressing high enough for Amine Gouiri to close down Jordan in midfield
key_matchups: Riyad Mahrez vs Husam Abu Dahab
Ehsan Haddad vs Algeria's wide/right-sided attacks
Musa Al-Taamari vs an outnumbered Algeria defensive line
phases.summary: The opening phase is played at a lively tempo, with both sides creating early moments from direct attacks and turnovers. Jordan are forced into deep defensive work before looking to counter, while Algeria show more control in possession and are quick to press, with Gouiri and Mahrez both threatening the Jordan back line. The match already features clear one-v-one and overload situations, including Jordan’s 3-on-1 counterattack that ends with a speculative shot from distance.

key_matchups: Riyad Mahrez vs Jordan back line
phases.summary: Jordan spent most of the phase in a compact 5-4-1, ceding possession and space while trying to block access into Algeria’s final third. Algeria controlled the ball patiently but initially struggled to penetrate, with Mahrez and Gouiri producing the best moments before Jordan threatened more on the break and then scored from a decisive transition attack through Al-Rashdan in the 36th minute.

formations_blocks: Algeria controlling possession but seemingly settling into a more cautious, game-management mode while trailing
Jordan defending a lead and making their time on the ball count more efficiently
adjustments: Algeria content to hold out for half-time despite being behind
Jordan protecting the 1-0 advantage after scoring in the 36th minute
phases.summary: At half-time, the tactical picture is one of Algeria having more of the ball but Jordan being sharper and more productive when they do attack. Algeria were content to see the first half out after falling behind, while Jordan’s opener gave them a lead they could defend going into the break.

formations_blocks: Jordan defending with five at the back
Algeria chasing the game and pressing higher
Algeria searching for space between the lines
adjustments: Algeria made two substitutions at 48' with Nabil Bentaleb and Nadhir Benbouali replacing Hicham Boudaoui and Ramiz Zerrouki
Jordan settled into a five-at-the-back shape to protect the lead
Algeria increased their attacking urgency after halftime
Jordan looked to transition quickly through Musa Al-Taamari and Ali Olwan
key_matchups: Riyad Mahrez delivery from a free kick vs Jordan goalkeeper Abulaila
Ibrahim Maza vs the Jordan midfield and right side of defence
Musa Al-Taamari vs the Algerian defensive line
Ali Olwan and Musa Al-Taamari combining against the Algeria defence
phases.summary: Algeria began the second half by chasing the game more aggressively, introducing Bentaleb and Benbouali and pushing to find gaps between Jordan’s lines. Jordan responded by dropping into a five-at-the-back block, absorbing pressure and threatening on the break, with both sides creating moments until Algeria finally found an equaliser through Benbouali in the 69th minute.

formations_blocks: Algeria in a game-management phase after taking the lead, slowing the game and banking back into a more protective shape
Jordan pushing forward late, with attacks down the right and pressure in the final minutes
Algeria defending compactly around the ball in the closing stages and forcing Jordan away from the final third
adjustments: Algeria replaced Mahrez with Anis Hadj Moussa for fresh legs and pace
Jordan replaced Mahmoud Al-Mardi with Odeh Al-Fakhouri to chase the game
Algeria later withdrew scorer Amine Gouiri and Rayan Aït-Nouri, bringing on Jaouen Hadjam and Zineddine Belaid to protect the lead
Algeria slowed the tempo and managed the clock after going ahead
Jordan moved the ball across the defensive line and into midfield late on but struggled to penetrate
key_matchups: Anis Hadj Moussa vs Jordan goalkeeper Abulaila, with Abulaila saving his shot before the resulting corner
Algeria’s attacking deliveries and crowding in the box vs Jordan’s offside/VAR scrutiny on the 82' goal
Jordan’s right-side break vs Algeria’s numbers around the ball near the corner flag
phases.summary: This phase was defined by Algeria’s late surge and subsequent game management after Gouiri’s 82nd-minute winner. After introducing fresh legs, Algeria threatened repeatedly from shots, corners, and free kicks, then dropped deeper and slowed the match to protect the lead. Jordan responded with late possession and a right-flank push, but Algeria’s compact defending and pressure around the ball kept them from finding a clear route back into the game.
